faqs.question: Q1. What devices/protocols are supported?
answer: Chromecast, AirPlay (Apple TV/AirPlay 2‑compatible TVs), LG webOS TVs, and most Miracast‑compatible devices. Phone and TV must share the same Wi‑Fi.

faqs.question: Q2. Do phone and TV have to be on the same Wi-Fi?
answer: Yes. Discovery and casting require the same router/subnet. Hotspots or client‑isolation networks may block discovery.

faqs.question: Q3. Why do you request Screen Capture and Microphone permissions?
answer: Screen Capture is the Android system authorization to mirror your display. Microphone permission enables audio mirroring on devices where system audio can’t be captured directly.

faqs.question: Q4. How do I enable or disable audio mirroring?
answer: Toggle Sound Mirroring before starting. Grant the mic permission when prompted. If you hear echo/noise, turn the toggle off to mirror video only.

faqs.question: Q5. Can I change resolution and frame rate?
answer: Yes. Set these before starting. Higher resolution is sharper but needs more bandwidth; higher frame rate is smoother but uses more battery and network.

faqs.question: Q6. My TV/device isn’t found. What should I do?
answer: Phone and TV on the same Wi-Fi; VPN/proxy disabled.
Router AP/client isolation is off; prefer 5 GHz.
TV has casting mode enabled (AirPlay/Miracast, etc.).
Reboot router, TV, and phone, then rescan.

faqs.question: Q7. Connected but the TV is black/no picture.
answer: Some apps block mirroring due to DRM or casting restrictions. Try other content (local media, photos, games) or another app that allows mirroring.

faqs.question: Q8. I see video but no sound.
answer: Enable Sound Mirroring and grant the mic permission before starting. Check TV volume/mute. Some content blocks audio capture.

faqs.question: Q9. High delay or stutter—how to improve?
answer: Use 5 GHz Wi-Fi, move closer to the router, reduce other network usage, lower resolution or frame rate, close heavy background apps, and reboot the router if needed.

faqs.question: Q10. Why do I get a PIN/pairing prompt?
answer: Some devices (e.g., AirPlay, certain TVs) require first‑time pairing. Enter the PIN shown on the TV or confirm on the TV screen.

faqs.question: Q11. How do I stop mirroring quickly?
answer: Pull down the notification shade and tap Stop Mirroring, or tap Stop on the app’s main screen.

faqs.question: Q12. What happens when Wi-Fi is unavailable?
answer: The app detects Wi-Fi status and guides you to connect to a network before scanning again.

faqs.question: Q13. Do you store my screen content?
answer: No. Mirroring data stays on your local network and is used only to cast to your TV. We don’t store or upload your screen.

faqs.question: Q14. Can I use other apps on my phone while mirroring?
answer: Yes, but DRM‑protected apps may block output. If you see throttling or lag, go to Home or lower quality and try again.

faqs.question: Q15. Does mirroring continue when the phone is locked?
answer: Some devices lower frame rate or stop in lock/power‑saving modes. Keep the screen awake or disable aggressive battery optimizations.
